zh.lifeSummary: 當William Gulielmus Wallenisis De Haia Ben Hait於1123年出生在法國諾門迪可騰時，他的父親William Hait The Flemish Sheriff of Pembroke De Haia Ben FitzPhillip43歲，母親Nest verch Rhys of Deheubarth38歲。他和Isabella Le'Gros FitzWilliam De'Windsor至少有2個兒子。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William Gulielmus Wallenisis De Haia Ben Hait于1123年出生在法国諾門迪可騰时，他的父亲William Hait The Flemish Sheriff of Pembroke De Haia Ben FitzPhillip43岁，母亲Nest verch Rhys of Deheubarth38岁。他和Isabella Le'Gros FitzWilliam De'Windsor至少有2个儿子。

nameMeanings.name: De
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Haia
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from any of various places in northern France named with Old Norse hagi ‘enclosure’, a word with cognates in most ancient Germanic languages. Compare Hay . The Haigs of Bemersyde in Berwickshire derive their name from the district of La Hague in Manche.
Scottish and Irish (Cavan): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Thaoig and Mac Thaidhg (see McCaig ).

variants.name: Hay
meanings: Scottish and English (northern; of Norman origin): habitational name from any of several places in Normandy called La Haie or La Haye (Old French haie ‘hedge, enclosure’, ‘forest for hunting deer and other animals’, a borrowing of the ancient Germanic word haga). Robert de Haia or de la Haye is known to have come from La Haye-du-Puits in Manche; he was the founder of Boxgrove Priory in Sussex (1123), and holder of the Honor of Halnaker (Sussex) and (by marriage) the barony of Kolswein (Lincolnshire). The Norman name was also taken to Ireland, where it has since flourished in the county of Wexford as Hay and Hayes . Elsewhere in Ireland the name usually has a native Irish origin, see below.

English: topographic name from Middle English hay(e), heye, heghe ‘enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg) or ‘forest fenced off for hunting’ (Old French haie); or else a habitational name from a place so called, such as Hay (in Herefordshire and Westmorland) or Hey in Scammonden (Yorkshire). It was no doubt sometimes synonymous with Hayward .
English: nickname for a tall man, from Middle English heigh, hey, high ‘high, tall’ (Old English hēah).

nameMeanings.name: Ben
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Native American (mainly Navajo) and apparently also African American: adoption of the English personal name Ben (short form of Benjamin ) as a surname.
Italian (also Del Ben): from a dialect form of the adverb bene ‘well’. This form, without the preposition, is found only in Belluno province, in particular in Taibon Agordino.

Indian (Gujarat): from Gujarati ben ‘sister’, from Sanskrit bhaginī, a title often attached to their personal name by Gujarati women. It is not a true surname, but is sometimes used as a surname by women who do not have a surname.

variants.name: Benjamin
meanings: Jewish (Sephardic and Ashkenazic), English, French, West Indian (mainly Haiti), and African (mainly Nigeria and Tanzania); Hungarian (Benjámin): from the Hebrew male personal name Binyāmīn ‘Son of the South’. In the Book of Genesis, it is treated as meaning ‘Son of the Right Hand’. The two senses are connected, since in Hebrew the south is thought of as the right-hand side of a person who is facing east. Benjamin was the youngest and favorite son of Jacob and supposed progenitor of one of the twelve tribes of Israel (Genesis 35:16-18; 42:4). The personal name was not common among Gentiles in the Middle Ages, but its use was sanctioned by virtue of having been borne by a Christian saint martyred in Persia in about AD 424. In some cases in medieval Europe it was also applied as a byname or nickname to the youngest (and beloved) son of a large family; this is the sense of modern French benjamin. In North America, this surname has absorbed cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Benyamin and Italian Beniamino.
History: John Benjamin (1598–1645) came from England to Watertown, MA, in 1632. Jean-Baptiste Benjamin dit Saint-Aubin from France married Jeanne Allard in QC in 1704.

variants.name: Fei
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Jia, Yanjun, Bin, Chen, Chen-Yu, Chiang, Guoping, Hong, Hua, Li Ping, Ling-Ling.
Chinese:

Mandarin form of the surname 費, meaning ‘fees’ in Chinese: (i) from Fei (費), the name of an ancient state (located in Shandong province), annexed during the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ). (ii) from Fei (費), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province), which was granted to Ji You, son of the Duke Huan of Lu (the 15th ruler of the state of Lu, died 694  BC ). (iii) from Fei (費), the name of a fief (located in Shandong province), which was granted to an official in the state of Lu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (iv) borne by descendants of Fei Wu Ji (費無級), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (v) adopted as a surname by the Fei Lian (費連) family from the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Hait
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Hyman, Gershon.
Jewish: variant of   Chait  .
Altered form of English   Hoyt  .

variants.name: Chait
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Arnon, Herschel, Isidor, Meyer, Nechama, Yossi.  German Frieda.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): occupational name for a tailor, from Yiddish khayet (Hebrew ḥayat).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

en.lifeSummary: When William Gulielmus Wallenisis De Haia Ben Hait was born in 1123, in Cotentin, Normandy, France, his father, William Hait The Flemish Sheriff of Pembroke De Haia Ben FitzPhillip, was 43 and his mother, Nest verch Rhys of Deheubarth, was 38. He had at least 2 sons with Isabella Le'Gros FitzWilliam De'Windsor.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William Gulielmus Wallenisis De Haia Ben Hait est né en 1123, à Cotentin, Normandie, France, son père, William Hait The Flemish Sheriff of Pembroke De Haia Ben FitzPhillip, avait 43 ans et sa mère, Nest verch Rhys of Deheubarth, avait 38 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils avec Isabella Le'Gros FitzWilliam De'Windsor.

de.lifeSummary: Als William Gulielmus Wallenisis De Haia Ben Hait 1123 in Cotentin, Normandie, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater William Hait The Flemish Sheriff of Pembroke De Haia Ben FitzPhillip 43 und seine Mutter Nest verch Rhys of Deheubarth 38. Er hatte mit Isabella Le'Gros FitzWilliam De'Windsor mindestens 2 Söhne.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William Gulielmus Wallenisis De Haia Ben Hait è nato il nel 1123 nel Cotentin, Normandie, Francia, il suo padre, William Hait The Flemish Sheriff of Pembroke De Haia Ben FitzPhillip, aveva 43 anni e il suo madre, Nest verch Rhys of Deheubarth, aveva 38 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli con Isabella Le'Gros FitzWilliam De'Windsor.

ja.lifeSummary: William Gulielmus Wallenisis De Haia Ben Haitが1123年にフランスノーマンディコテンティンで生まれた時，父William Hait The Flemish Sheriff of Pembroke De Haia Ben FitzPhillipは43歳，母Nest verch Rhys of Deheubarthは38歳であった。彼はIsabella Le'Gros FitzWilliam De'Windsorとの間に少なくとも2人の息子をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: William Gulielmus Wallenisis De Haia Ben Hait(이)가 1123년 프랑스 노먼디 컷틴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Hait The Flemish Sheriff of Pembroke De Haia Ben FitzPhillip(은)는 43세였고, 그의 어머니 Nest verch Rhys of Deheubarth(은)는 38세였다. 그는 Isabella Le'Gros FitzWilliam De'Windsor(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William Gulielmus Wallenisis De Haia Ben Hait nasceu em 1123, em Cotentin, Normandie, França, seu pai, William Hait The Flemish Sheriff of Pembroke De Haia Ben FitzPhillip, tinha 43 anos e sua mãe, Nest verch Rhys of Deheubarth, tinha 38 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos com Isabella Le'Gros FitzWilliam De'Windsor.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Gulielmus Wallenisis De Haia Ben Hait родился в 1123 г. в местности котентинь, норманди, Франция, его отцу по имени William Hait The Flemish Sheriff of Pembroke De Haia Ben FitzPhillip было 43 года, а матери, которую звали Nest verch Rhys of Deheubarth, 38. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына от Isabella Le'Gros FitzWilliam De'Windsor.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William Gulielmus Wallenisis De Haia Ben Hait nació en 1123, en Cotentin, Normandie, Francia, su papá, William Hait The Flemish Sheriff of Pembroke De Haia Ben FitzPhillip, tenía 43 años y su mamá, Nest verch Rhys of Deheubarth, tenía 38 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos con Isabella Le'Gros FitzWilliam De'Windsor.
